commands.review_summary:
The PR removes a content-script daemon-token preflight for hover summaries and adds focused tests for starting hover requests without a stored token.

Reproducibility: yes. from source inspection: current main exits before sending hover:summarize when hover summaries are enabled but the stored token is empty. A full browser reproduction would be loading the extension with Direct provider/Ollama configured, no daemon token stored, and hovering a public link.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] No ClawSweeper repair is needed; the next action is contributor-provided real behavior proof plus normal maintainer review.

target.title: [codex] fix direct hover without daemon token
target.body:
## Summary

- allow hover-enabled content scripts to ask the background for a summary even when no daemon token is stored
- keep token requirements in the background controller, where daemon mode is already distinguished from direct provider mode
- add focused coverage that hover preflight does not treat daemon-token presence as a prerequisite

## Root cause

The hover content script returned early when `settings.token` was empty. That was correct for daemon-backed hover summaries, but wrong for direct provider/Ollama hover summaries because the background controller can run those without a daemon token.

## Validation

- `pnpm vitest run tests/hover.security.test.ts tests/chrome.model-routing.test.ts`
- `pnpm format:check`
- `pnpm lint`
- `pnpm typecheck`
target.author: vincent-peng
